channel.title: Architecture on Cyril's Cloud & AI Daily
channel.description: Recent content in Architecture on Cyril's Cloud & AI Daily

item.title: Improving token efficiency in GitHub Agentic Workflows
item.description:
There’s been an important development in the GitHub Copilot space that caught my attention, and I wanted to break it down — not just what was announced, but why it matters and how it fits into the bigger picture.

What’s New

GitHub Agentic Workflows is like a team of street sweepers that clean up little messes in your repo. These teams significantly improve repo hygiene and quality, but as with all agentic work, cost is a growing concern for developers. And because CI jobs like agentic workflows are automatically scheduled and triggered, costs can accumulate out of view.

item.title: Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb)
item.description:
There’s been an important development in the Microsoft Foundry space that caught my attention, and I wanted to break it down — not just what was announced, but why it matters and how it fits into the bigger picture.

What’s New

We’re excited to announce that theAzure MCP Serveris now available as anMCP Bundle(.mcpb). This means you can install the Azure MCP Server intoClaude Desktopand other MCP-compatible clients with minimum setup—no Node.js, Python, or .NET runtime required.

item.title: Chat History Storage Patterns in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
There’s been an important development in the Azure OpenAI & GPT Models space that caught my attention, and I wanted to break it down — not just what was announced, but why it matters and how it fits into the bigger picture.

What’s New

When people talk about building AI agents, they usually focus on models, tools, and prompts. In practice, one of the most important architectural decisions is much simpler:where does the conversation history live?
